description:
You are the system administrator responsible for two small internal services that store their runtime settings in text-based configuration files.  
Both configuration files already exist but must be updated to match the new deployment policy.  

FILES AND LOCATIONS  
1. YAML file:  
   /home/user/webapp/config/settings.yaml  

2. TOML file:  
   /home/user/monitor/service.toml  

3. Summary log (create if it does not exist):  
   /home/user/update_report.txt  

STARTING STATE (what you will find on disk before you begin)  
1. /home/user/webapp/config/settings.yaml currently contains:  
   ```
   version: 1.0
   debug: false
   database:
     host: localhost
     port: 5432
   ```
2. /home/user/monitor/service.toml currently contains:  
   ```
   [server]
   port = 9000

   [logging]
   level = "debug"
   ```

REQUIRED FINAL STATE  
After your edits, the files must look **exactly** like the snippets that follow (including indentation, quotation marks, and blank lines).  An automated test will perform a byte-for-byte comparison, so be precise.

1. /home/user/webapp/config/settings.yaml (YAML)  
   ```
   version: 1.0
   debug: true
   database:
     host: localhost
     port: 5432
   allowed_hosts:
     - example.com
     - api.example.com
   ```

2. /home/user/monitor/service.toml (TOML)  
   ```
   [server]
   port = 8080

   [logging]
   level = "info"
   ```

3. /home/user/update_report.txt (plain text log created by you)  
   It must contain exactly three lines (no extra whitespace).  
   Line 1: Updated settings.yaml – enabled debug and added allowed_hosts  
   Line 2: Updated service.toml – set port to 8080 and log level to info  
   Line 3: Configuration update complete ✅  

CONSTRAINTS & HINTS  
• You do NOT have root access; stay within /home/user.  
• Perform the edits with any standard shell tools (e.g., echo, printf, sed, tee, cat, etc.).  
• Use only 2–3 shell commands in total; combine actions whenever reasonable.  
• Remember to create /home/user/update_report.txt with exactly the three lines shown above.  
• When finished, both configuration files and the log must match the “REQUIRED FINAL STATE” verbatim.
